items.title: FA Cup: Wrexham to host Chelsea in fifth round
items.summary: Wrexham will host Chelsea in the fifth round of the FA Cup.

items.title: Luis Enrique slams 'worthless' Dembélé PSG take
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique said he will not allow any player to be bigger than the club ahead of Tuesday's Champions League knockout phase playoff first leg against AS Monaco.

items.title: Haji Wright hat trick puts Coventry top of table
items.summary: Haji Wright hit a hat trick as Coventry returned to the top of the Sky Bet Championship with a 3-1 home victory over in-form promotion rivals Middlesbrough.

items.title: Fàbregas hits out at his own striker Morata for red
items.summary: Como coach Cesc Fàbregas did not hold back when criticising Álvaro Morata after the veteran forward was sent off.

items.title: Own-goal ends dream FA Cup run for Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield were unable to recreate the magic of last month's FA Cup win over Crystal Palace, with a Sam Heathcote own goal sending Brentford through to the fifth round after a spirited contest at Moss Rose.

items.title: Man Utd consider swoop for Mac Allister - Tuesday's gossip
items.summary: Man Utd consider swoop for Liverpool's Alexis Mac Allister, Man City not keen on James Trafford sale, Nicolas Jackson to return to Chelsea from Bayern Munich.

items.title: Barcelona verspeelt koppositie met derbynederlaag bij Girona
items.summary: Barcelona heeft maandagavond zijn vierde competitienederlaag van het seizoen geleden. Vlak voor tijd ging het voor de ploeg van Hansi Flick mis in de derby tegen Girona. Real Madrid gaat nu alleen aan kop in La Liga.

items.title: Wright fires Coventry back to top of Championship with win over Middlesbrough!
items.summary: Highlights of the Sky Bet Championship clash between Coventry City and Middlesbrough.

items.title: Haji Wright hat-trick sinks Middlesbrough and sends Coventry back to top of Championship
items.summary: “We are top of the league,” sang the Coventry City supporters of returning to the summit of the Championship. Ironically, it was a result that quelled the nagging noise surrounding Frank Lampard and his team. Coventry, pace-setters for the majority of the season, had won just four league games since the end of November. But Haji Wright hit a timely hat-trick as Coventry again traded places with Middlesbrough, whose six-game winning run came to an abrupt halt. Riley McGree pulled a goal back midway through the second half but from the restart Boro conceded a penalty that allowed Wright to claim the match ball. Coventry’s lead may be a single point but this felt a significant victory – psychologically as much as anything – their having taken just 16 points from the previous available 39. Continue reading...

items.title: Sky Sports Golf Podcast: Morikawa edges Pebble Beach thriller
items.summary: Listen to the latest episode of the Sky Sports Golf Podcast as Nick Dougherty joins the show to reflect on Collin Morikawa's win at the Pebble Beach Pro-Am.

items.title: 'Big players, big moments, big nights!' | Wright completes hat-trick from penalty spot
items.summary: Haji Wright completed his hat-trick for Coventry City from the penalty spot to put the Sky Blues 3-1 ahead against Middlesbrough in their Sky Bet Championship clash.

items.title: Cambuur klopt Jong Ajax in slotfase en hervat jacht op ADO
items.summary: SC Cambuur heeft de draad weer opgepakt in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie en het Jong Ajax van de debuterende trainer Oscar García verslagen. Het venijn zat in de staart, waarbij Cambuur als winnaar uit de bus kwam. Ook bij Jong FC Utrecht - Willem II viel de beslissing in de slotfase van de wedstrijd.

items.title: Macclesfield’s fairytale FA Cup run ended by Heathcote’s own goal against Brentford
items.summary: For 160 minutes this season, Macclesfield had been more than a match for illustrious Premier League opponents in the FA Cup. Ultimately it took an unfortunate own goal from Sam Heathcote, a PE teacher when not playing as a part-timer in the sixth tier, to put Brentford into the fifth round. As cruel as it felt on Heathcote and Macclesfield, they can once again be proud of their performance. Crystal Palace could not break down the Cheshire team in the previous round and for 70 minutes Brentford had also struggled for a route to goal. The non-league side were even able to rouse themselves to push for a late equaliser, rather than cave in to Keith Andrews’s men. Continue reading...

items.title: Eigen goal van gymleraar maakt einde aan FA Cup-sprookje Macclesfield
items.summary: Het FA Cup-avontuur van Macclesfield FC is maandagavond ten einde gekomen. De ploeg van het zesde niveau in de Engelse voetbalpiramide slaagde er bij momenten in om Premier League-club Brentford te laten bibberen, maar ging door een eigen doelpunt toch kopje onder. Brentford bekert door en treft nu West Ham United.

items.title: Sneijder: 'De ene keer excelleert Godts, de andere keer is het een drama'
items.summary: Hoewel Mika Godts clubtopscorer is van Ajax, is er volgens Wesley Sneijder nog veel werk aan de winkel. De oud-international vindt dat de Belg veel constanter moet worden.

items.title: Curry: England ready to attack Ireland - we have to be better
items.summary: Tom Curry admits England were "fighting with one hand behind our back" during Saturday's defeat to Scotland but insists the team is ready to mount a response against Ireland.

items.title: Transfers Ajax: Jordi Cruijff zocht contact met Sneijder
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Ajax op de voet, ook nu de winterse transferwindow gesloten is. De doelwitten van de Amsterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Will we see changes to first England squad in 2026?
items.summary: The Lionesses return to competitive action next month as their campaign to qualify for the 2027 Women's World Cup begins. So what changes could we see?

items.title: FA Cup fifth-round draw: Mansfield v Arsenal, Wrexham v Chelsea – as it happened
items.summary: Wrexham and Mansfield host big guns in round five while Newcastle face Manchester City and Liverpool go to Wolves TNT have kicked off with a walk-and-talk around a packed Macclesfield dressing room, the only problem being that the camera lens keeps steaming up. Let’s get on with it, shall we? Three minutes until the draw, according to an on-screen countdown that will inevitably prove to be inaccurate. I think the fifth round is my personal favourite round of the Cup; close enough to Wembley but still with plenty of room for surprises. Continue reading...

items.title: League One Mansfield to host Arsenal in fifth round - full FA Cup draw
items.summary: League One club Mansfield will host record 14-time winners Arsenal at Field Mill in the FA Cup fifth round, as Manchester City head to Newcastle in a repeat of the League Cup semi-final.
